title: Align Grafana Cloud k6 metrics with application metrics in Explore

blocks.content:
Grafana Cloud k6 exposes finished test metrics to Grafana through the `grafanacloud-k6` data source so you can query virtual users, iteration rate, and HTTP error series in **Explore** alongside application metrics that live in your Prometheus-compatible store. Refer to [Correlate results in Grafana](/docs/grafana-cloud/testing/k6/analyze-results/correlate-results-in-grafana/) for screenshots of the **Explore** entry points from Grafana Cloud k6.

This milestone only proves both sides resolve on one timeline. You do not need a spike in flight yet. Pick any recent test run if one exists, or leave run filters empty and confirm the data source responds without errors.

To align k6 and application metrics, complete the following steps.

1. Open **Explore** from the main navigation.

1. Add a query and select the `grafanacloud-k6` data source.

   Grafana Cloud uses this name for Grafana Cloud k6 metrics.

1. Choose **Metrics** as the query type and pick your Performance **Project**.

1. Select a **Test** and **Test run**, or leave **Test run** empty to browse available metrics.

1. Run the query.

   Confirm you see a table or graph instead of a configuration error.

1. Click **Add query** and select your Prometheus-compatible data source.

1. Run PromQL that targets the quickpizza workload.

   On some Grafana Cloud demo environments application series include `namespace="k6-quickpizza"`. Substitute the namespace, job, or service labels your administrator documents.

1. Set the **Explore** time picker to the same absolute window for both queries.

   For example, use the last one to six hours. Confirm both queries still return data. You reuse this alignment when you compare probes, logs, and traces with your spike window.

Check your work: you can move on when `grafanacloud-k6` returns metrics without errors, your metrics query returns quickpizza application series for the same window, and you know how to add a second query in **Explore**. If either data source fails, open the troubleshooting topics for this milestone.

In the next milestone you prepare a live layout so you can watch virtual users and application traffic during the spike.
